title: Daniel Yergin Sees a 'Different World' Emerging After the Hormuz Crisis
snippet: When it comes to the history of oil and energy, nobody is more famous or well respected than Daniel Yergin. He is the Vice Chairman of S&P Global, and the Pulitzer Prize winning author of both The Prize: The Epic Quest…
contentText: When it comes to the history of oil and energy, nobody is more famous or well respected than Daniel Yergin. He is the Vice Chairman of S&P Global, and the Pulitzer Prize winning author of both The Prize: The Epic Quest for Oil, Money, and Power and The New Map: Energy, Climate, and the Clash of Nations. So we had to get his insights on the war in Iran, and its historical significance. Iran's ability to control the Straight against a much stronger military is a demonstration that the balance of global power is changing, with profound ramifications for countries around the world. We discuss how different regions are being affected, and how it will change their calculus when it comes to energy security. We also talk about the AI industry's seemingly insatiable demand for electricity, and how…

title: Iran Reports 'Some Sign' US Ready to Break Blockade | Daybreak Europe 4/22/2026
snippet: Bloomberg Daybreak Europe is your essential morning viewing to stay ahead. Live from London, we set the agenda for your day, catching you up with overnight markets news from the US and Asia. On today's show, Iran’s semi…
contentText: Bloomberg Daybreak Europe is your essential morning viewing to stay ahead. Live from London, we set the agenda for your day, catching you up with overnight markets news from the US and Asia. On today's show, Iran’s semi-official Tasnim news reports that there's been 'some sign' that the US is ready to end its blockade of Iran's ports. Brent crude, the dollar and US Treasury yields all dipped on the report. The US President has extended the ceasefire with Iran indefinitely. Tehran has so far refused to take part in a second round of peace talks. Bloomberg understands that unauthorized users have gained access to Mythos, Anthropic's most powerful, and potentially dangerous, AI model. Plus Donald Trump's pick to become the next chair of the Federal Reserve, Kevin Warsh, has promised to be in…

title: Blog Review: Apr. 22
snippet: Coverage closure; EM sim for AMS; CXL 4; root of trust for ATMs. The post Blog Review: Apr. 22 appeared first on Semiconductor Engineering.
contentText: In a podcast, Siemens EDA’s Harry Foster and Vladislav Palfy chat about why coverage closure has become one of the biggest bottlenecks in modern verification and how a unified approach that combines planning, automation, and analytics helps teams break through coverage plateaus. Synopsys’ Emily Gerken and Marc Swinnen consider the challenges of designing analog and mixed-signal circuits at advanced nodes and why electromagnetic simulation is needed to extract accurate resistance, inductance, and capacitance or S-parameter models. Cadence’s Sangeeta Soni finds that while CXL 4.0 takes a major leap forward in bandwidth, scalability, and system flexibility, it also introduces substantial verification complexity and suggests verification IP to reduce risk. Keysight’s D…

title: TSV Complexity Leads To Manufacturing Bottleneck
snippet: Creating through-silicon vias is a necessary but daunting challenge. The post TSV Complexity Leads To Manufacturing Bottleneck appeared first on Semiconductor Engineering.
contentText: Key Takeaways: Through-silicon vias are the biggest enabler of 3D chip stacking and chip-to-PCB connections through silicon interposers. The AI boom is causing HBM and advanced assembly shortages, straining the supply chain. Optimization around etch, fill and reveal help reduce TSV cost. Through-silicon vias (TSVs) provide essential interconnects between DRAM dies inside high-bandwidth memory stacks, silicon interposers, and emerging 3D chip stacks, but as TSV dimensions shrink they are becoming increasingly expensive to fabricate and prone to errors. TSVs are necessary for integrating MEMS, RF, analog ICs, GPUs, and more. They enhance the performance of individual dies and act as vertical transmission lines for high-frequency electrical signals, thereby improving multi-die systems. But a…

title: Deutsche Telekom Said to Weigh T-Mobile Combination
snippet: Deutsche Telekom is considering a full combination with its American arm T-Mobile US, a move that would create a multinational telecom group and rank as the largest-ever public M&A deal, people with knowledge of the mat…
contentText: Deutsche Telekom is considering a full combination with its American arm T-Mobile US, a move that would create a multinational telecom group and rank as the largest-ever public M&A deal, people with knowledge of the matter said. Bloomberg's Manuel Baigorri reports. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Anthropic’s Mythos Accessed by Unauthorized Users
snippet: A small group of unauthorized users have accessed Anthropic's new Mythos AI model, a technology that the company says is so powerful it can enable dangerous cyberattacks, according to a person familiar with the matter a…
contentText: A small group of unauthorized users have accessed Anthropic's new Mythos AI model, a technology that the company says is so powerful it can enable dangerous cyberattacks, according to a person familiar with the matter and documentation viewed by Bloomberg News. Bloomberg Intelligence's Matt Bloxham explains. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: SpaceX is working with Cursor and has an option to buy the startup for $60B
snippet: The move could shore up weaknesses at each company, but it also reveals them. Neither Cursor nor xAI has proprietary models that can match the leading offerings from Anthropic and OpenAI — the same companies now competi…
contentText: The move could shore up weaknesses at each company, but it also reveals them. Neither Cursor nor xAI has proprietary models that can match the leading offerings from Anthropic and OpenAI — the same companies now competing directly with Cursor for the developer market.

title: Adobe Expands Agentic AI Ecosystem With Big Tech Partners
snippet: Adobe is launching a new agentic platform targeting business-to-business and consumers that, in addition to working closely with model providers OpenAI and Anthropic, can ease concern about disruption to its business. T…
contentText: Adobe is launching a new agentic platform targeting business-to-business and consumers that, in addition to working closely with model providers OpenAI and Anthropic, can ease concern about disruption to its business. The company is partnering with tech giants Amazon, Anthropic, Google, Nvidia, OpenAI, and more to build what it calls the broadest agentic AI ecosystem in the industry. Anil Chakravarthy, Adobe Customer Experience Orchestration Business President, joins Bloomberg Businessweek Daily from the Adobe Summit 2026 in Las Vegas to discuss. He speaks with Carol Massar and Tim Stenovec. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: SpaceX Has Deal for Right to Acquire Cursor for $60 Billion
snippet: SpaceX said it has an agreement giving it the right to acquire artificial intelligence startup Cursor for $60 billion later this year or to pay $10 billion for the companies’ work together, part of the Elon Musk-run fir…
contentText: SpaceX said it has an agreement giving it the right to acquire artificial intelligence startup Cursor for $60 billion later this year or to pay $10 billion for the companies’ work together, part of the Elon Musk-run firm’s efforts to catch up with rivals in AI coding tools.

title: Stocks Extend Drop, Trump Extends Iran Ceasefire | The Close 4/21/2026
snippet: Bloomberg Television brings you the latest news and analysis leading up to the final minutes and seconds before and after the closing bell on Wall Street. Today's guests are Former NY Federal Reserve President Bill Dudl…
contentText: Bloomberg Television brings you the latest news and analysis leading up to the final minutes and seconds before and after the closing bell on Wall Street. Today's guests are Former NY Federal Reserve President Bill Dudley, Niles Investment Management’s Daniel Niles, Rosenblatt Securities’ Barton Crockett, Former USAA CEO Wayne Peacock, Gabelli’s Tony Bancroft, DWS Group’s David Bianco, BD8 Capital Partners CIO Barbara Doran, Eurasia Group’s Jonathan Lieber, PGIM’s Gregory Peters, LossDog Co-CEO Tom Sosnoff, & The Planetary Society Chief Ambassador Bill Nye. (Source: Bloomberg)
